title: Fractionation of Light and Heavy Mitochondria by Gradient Cushion using the FOCUS™ Mitochondria Kit

guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ft9lb","text":"INTRODUCTION FOCUS™","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ha7g","text":"Mitochondria kit enables the fast and easy isolation of enriched mitochondrial fractions from animal cells and tissues. The majority of the isolated mitochondria obtained from this kit contain intact inner and outer membranes. This kit contains reagents for processing 50-80 preparations of 20 million cultured mammalian cells or 20-30 preps of 50-100mg tissue. The number of preparations varies depending on the protocol, preparation size and cell or tissue type.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6f2bb","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fnbd9","text":"ITEM(S) SUPPLIED (Cat. #: 786-022)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4q73","text":"   Description Size   SubCell Buffer-I 60ml   SubCell Buffer-II [3X] 30ml   SubCell Buffer-IV 25ml   SubCell Buffer-V 15ml   Mitochondria Storage Buffer 10ml   Mitochondria Storage Component 1 vial    ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":11},{"style":"bold","offset":15,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":201}],"data":[]},{"key":"lebl","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cuki","text":"STORAGE CONDITION","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cr6qu","text":"The kit is shipped at ambient temperature. After receiving store the kit components at 4°C except Mitochondria Storage Component at -20°C. The kit is stable for one year when stored unopened. Use aseptic techniques when handling the reagent solutions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1vaeq","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6pcbu","text":"ADDITIONAL ITEMS REQUIRED","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bfp5s","text":"Syringes and 20 gauge needles or Wheaton Dounce homogenizer, centrifuge and centrifuge tubes. Optional reagents: Delipidated BSA, Trypsin, PBS and protease inhibitor cocktail.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"41ip1","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dcvss","text":"PREPARATION BEFORE USE","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"denac","text":"All buffers should be kept ice cold. Dilute appropriate volume of 3X SubCell Buffer-II to 1X with SubCell Buffer-I as needed (e.g. mix 2ml SubCell Buffer-I with 1ml SubCell Buffer-II). All centrifugation steps should be performed at 4°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e43jv","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3m7ji","text":"Preparation of Working Mitochondria Storage Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":50}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d3hg2","text":"Pipette 0.5ml Mitochondria Storage Buffer to Mitochondria Storage Component vial. Pipette up and down a few times to dissolve all components completely. Transfer the solution of Mitochondria Storage Component to Mitochondria Storage Buffer bottle and mix well. The Working Mitochondria Storage Buffer should be kept frozen for long-term use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"35pt0","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"32b3o","text":"Solubilization of Mitochondrial Fractions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fllna","text":"The fractionated mitochondria may be solubilized in any suitable buffer consistent with downstream procedures. FOCUS/Extraction Buffer-VI (Cat. # 786-233) is suitable for solubilization of mitochondria fractions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":""},"colWidths":[200,55],"data":[["\u003cstrong\u003eDescription\u003c/strong\u003e","\u003cstrong\u003eSize\u003c/strong\u003e"],["SubCell Buffer-I","60ml"],["SubCell Buffer-II [3X]","30ml"],["SubCell Buffer-IV","25ml"],["SubCell Buffer-V","15ml"],["Mitochondria Storage Buffer","10ml"],["Mitochondria Storage Component","1 vial\u0026nbsp;"]],"guid":"82A25D40458011E9A24D69A8AA1320D3","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"e3dga","text":"All buffers should be kept ice cold. Dilute appropriate volume of 3X SubCell Buffer-II to 1X with SubCell Buffer-I as needed (e.g. mix 2ml SubCell Buffer-I with 1ml SubCell Buffer-II). All centrifugation steps should be performed at 4°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Suspend the mitochondrial pellet in 100µl 1X SubCell Buffer-II.
Make a step gradient by adding 200µl SubCell Buffer-V to a centrifuge tube and then overlaying with 200µl SubCell Buffer-IV.
Gently float the mitochondrial suspension on the surface of the step gradient.

Centrifuge the gradient at 20,000x g for 20 minutes. Observe the two white bands. 00:20:00 The band at the interface of SubCell Buffer-IV and V is the heavy mitochondria fraction, whereas the band above the heavy mitochondria band is the light mitochondria fraction.
Carefully remove each band to a separate tube.
Dilute the mitochondrial suspensions with equal volume of 1X SubCell Buffer-II.
Centrifuge the tubes at 12,000x g for 15 minutes and discard the supernatant. 00:15:00

Suspend the mitochondrial pellets in 30-50µl Working Mitochondria Storage Buffer and keep the suspensions on ice before downstream processing. The suspensions may be stored on ice up to 48 hours. Freezing and thawing may compromise mitochondrial integrity.
creator: Colin Heath
